programme.title: Black '47
programme.desc: In 1847 an Irish mercenary fighting for the British army abandons his post to seek revenge on those responsible for the death of his family.

programme.title: Freeway Killer
programme.desc: Californian serial killer William Bonin (Scott Anthony Leet) searches for victims on the state's highways.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Entrance
programme.desc: Things lurking on the periphery of people's lives lead to horrific conclusions.

programme.title: The Dry
programme.desc: A federal agent investigates the decades-old death of a teenage girl in his drought-stricken hometown.

programme.title: The Beta Test
programme.desc: A married Hollywood agent receives a mysterious letter for an anonymous sexual encounter and becomes ensnared in a sinister world of lying, murder and infidelity.

programme.title: The Vigil
programme.desc: Terror strikes when an Orthodox Jewish man agrees to watch over the dead body of a community member in a dilapidated Brooklyn home.

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: Two black American social scientists pose as bank robbers to study racial dynamics in small-town law enforcement.

programme.title: Ride
programme.desc: Struggling actor James pays his bills by driving for a ride-sharing service in Los Angeles. His night soon takes a shocking turn when Bruno, armed with a gun, takes James and a woman on a terrifying, white-knuckle trip that spirals out of control.

programme.title: The Stanford Prison Experiment
programme.desc: In 1971, Stanford's Professor Philip Zimbardo (Billy Crudup) conducts a controversial psychology experiment in which college students pretend to be either prisoners or guards, but the proceedings soon get out of hand.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: Queen of Earth
programme.desc: Tensions rise when an emotionally fragile woman (Elisabeth Moss) spends one week with her best friend (Katherine Waterston).

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: The Invoking
programme.desc: Samantha inherits a house from family she never knew and goes to the property with three friends to see it. Shortly after their arrival, Samantha experiences horrific visions as the demons from her past refuse to stay buried.

programme.title: The Invoking 2
programme.desc: Waking nightmares, bloodthirsty spirits, demonic forces, and vengeful poltergeists visit horror upon their victims in this anthology.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.title: Don't Go
programme.desc: Devastated by his daughter's death in a terrible accident, a man becomes convinced that he can bring her back through a secret that is hidden within a recurring dream.

programme.title: Apartment 4E
programme.desc: Trapped in a dark world of her own making, Piper (Nicole Beharie) has not left her apartment in a long time. A knock on her door reveals a stranger who may have answers to the mystery at the center of her world.

programme.title: Entrance
programme.desc: Things lurking on the periphery of people's lives lead to horrific conclusions.

programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland

programme.title: Sanam Teri Kasam
programme.desc: When an awkward librarian gets disowned by her family, she unexpectedly finds support from her mysterious, brooding neighbor, sparking an unlikely friendship-and maybe more.

programme.title: Aranmanai 4
programme.desc: Daniel's quest to uncover his sister's death spirals into a nightmare, revealing sinister truths and igniting a chain of terror that changes him forever.

programme.title: Saaho
programme.desc: An undercover agent and his partner go after a thief who has stolen 2,000 crore rupees. Soon, they realise that the case is linked to the death of a crime lord and an emerging gang war.

programme.title: Raksha Bandhan
programme.desc: Lala Kedarnath vows to marry off his four sisters before anything else, juggling family obligations and his chat shop, while putting his childhood crush, Sapna, on hold.

programme.title: Antim: The Final Truth
programme.desc: A poor farmer's son moves to the city and becomes a fearless gangster, clashing with an upright policeman determined to take him down, leading to an intense battle of wills.

programme.title: Kanjoos Makhichoos
programme.desc: Man saves money for father's Char Dham Yatra dream, tragedy strikes in floods.

programme.title: Oh My God
programme.desc: A shopkeeper takes god to court when his shop is destroyed by an earthquake.

programme.title: Yamla Pagla Deewana 2
programme.desc: The trio is back with double the fun, double the action and double the romance!

programme.title: Mera Badla - Revenge
programme.desc: Family haunted after winning portrait prize seeks Acharya and Dr. Vijay's aid.

programme.title: Makkhi
programme.desc: Sudeep, obsessed with Bindu, kills her lover Jani, who is reborn as a fly. Jani and Bindu team up to exact revenge on Sudeep in creative ways.

programme.title: Indian
programme.desc: DCP Raj Shekhar Azad nabs terrorist Wasim Khan, but uncovers deep police corruption involving his father-in-law. Framed for murder, he must clear his name and fight for justice.

programme.title: Krack
programme.desc: Three notorious criminals clash with Potharaju Shankar, a hot-tempered cop, in a series of intense confrontations that ultimately lead them behind bars.

programme.title: Krantiveer
programme.desc: Pratap is guided by journalist Megha Dixit to empower his village, aiming for independence and self-reliance. They work together to spark local change and resilience.

programme.title: Big Dhamaka
programme.desc: Trouble ensues when two lookalikes from varying backgrounds fall for the same girl. However, an unsuspecting connection between the duplicates further complicates their situation.

programme.title: Zeher
programme.desc: Siddharth, a soon-to-be-divorced police officer, embarks on an affair with Anna. When she's found dead, he confronts unforeseen consequences, unraveling a web of deceit and danger.

programme.title: Article 15
programme.desc: In the rural heartlands of India, an upright police officer sets out on a crusade against violent caste-based crimes and discrimination.

programme.title: Lingaa
programme.desc: Lingaa, once a petty thief, reforms after learning his grandfather built a village dam. He dedicates himself to protecting the dam from a corrupt minister's destructive plans.

programme.title: Suriya S3
programme.desc: Singham returns to confront a powerful toxic-waste tycoon, risking everything to protect citizens. Can he overcome hurdles and deliver justice for the city's health scares?

programme.title: Krrish
programme.desc: In a wild ride of love, betrayal, and superhuman powers, Krishna must navigate a dangerous web of deceit and manipulation orchestrated by those craving ultimate power and control.

programme.title: Dabangg 3
programme.desc: Chulbul Pandey, now a formidable police superintendent, faces personal danger as old rival Bali Singh resurfaces, threatening his family amid escalating violence and confrontation.

programme.title: Badal
programme.desc: 1984 riots victim joins terrorist group, seeking revenge for family's death.

programme.title: Radhe
programme.desc: After taking the dreaded gangster Gani Bhai, ACP Rajveer Shikawat aka Radhe goes on a manhunt to find the wealthiest man of the town secretly running a crime syndicate.

programme.title: The Reunion
programme.desc: In 1997, three prep school friends hide a dark secret. In 2022, a gym wall is set to be torn down-ready to reveal the truth buried for years. Mystery is about to unfold.

programme.title: Crocodile Island
programme.desc: Stranded survivors on a mysterious island battle ancient crocodiles and uncover dark secrets, racing against time to escape the deadly predators and the cursed land.

programme.title: Snake Cave
programme.desc: When a little farm girl gets trapped in the lies of a sinister stranger, she must fight with the truth to save her family.

programme.title: Crew
programme.desc: Three diligent women find their lives entangled in a web of deceit, facing unwarranted predicaments as their destinies unexpectedly collide.

programme.title: Waah Zindagi
programme.desc: A struggling farmer's son tries to become an entrepreneur and win over his childhood love.

programme.title: Buddha in a Traffic Jam
programme.desc: A management student's social media success stirs ideological conflicts.

programme.title: Mere Pyare Prime Minister
programme.desc: Kannu writes a letter to the prime minister of India after his mother is raped.

programme.title: Daddy
programme.desc: Arun Gawli becomes one of India's most notable gangsters before he ventures into politics.

programme.title: Vicky Donor
programme.desc: Vicky works as a sperm donor for Dr Baldev Chaddha and boosts the clinic's success.

programme.title: Dil Juunglee
programme.desc: Love blossoms between Koroli and Sumit when the latter joins her English class.

programme.title: Kisko Tha Pata
programme.desc: Shreya calls off her wedding with her boyfriend after a stranger predicts tragedy.

programme.title: Manmarziyaan
programme.desc: A young, free-spirited woman is torn between two very different men and must decide who to marry.

programme.title: Toofaan
programme.desc: An orphan meets a young woman who encourages him to pursue his passion for boxing.

programme.title: Bhakshak
programme.desc: A journalist begins an investigation into abuse being covered up at a girls' shelter.

programme.title: Mission Majnu
programme.desc: A spy takes on a mission to expose a covert nuclear weapons program in the heart of Pakistan.

programme.title: Welcome to New York
programme.desc: A recovery agent and a designer become part of a big Bollywood event in New York.

programme.title: Turtle
programme.desc: Ramkaran churns the dry earth of a village in search of water to quench his thirst.

programme.title: Kites
programme.desc: Jay, a dance instructor, falls in love with a powerful man's fiancée.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The last of the eight quarter-finalists return to play their first of a maximum of three matches in this round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three neuroscientists hailing from Australia, South Africa and Canada scrum down against the intellectual brawn of three rugby fans, as they compete to draw together the connections between many disparate elements that, at first glance, seem utterly bereft of associations. So what connects cream, quotation marks, tennis matches and yellow lines?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In December 2015, Tim Peake became Britain’s first astronaut on board the International Space Station. For two years, he had been filming a video diary for Horizon as he prepared to leave. From family life to the rigorous training, this is an intimate portrait and remarkable insight into the world of an astronaut.

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven
programme.desc: After the latest raid by bandits, the villagers in a small settlement in Mexico band together to hire a group of disparate gunfighters to protect them.

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson in conversation with stage and screen star Yul Brynner. Featuring film clips from The Ten Commandments, The King and I, The Magnificent Seven and Catlow.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes considers how the Civil War industrialised society, bringing steam power, the Brooklyn Bridge, plutocrats and an urban underclass. Nostalgia for loss entered the culture and brought the Museum Age.

programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 22 June 1990 and featuring Magnum, Roxette, Big Fun & Sonia, Wilson Phillips, Yazz, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Luciano Pavarotti and Elton John.

programme.title: Elvis Presley: '68 Comeback Special
programme.desc: In this remastered and extended concert recording, Elvis struts his stuff in his own charming and inimitable style, performing a massive collection of his hits, including Hound Dog, All Shook Up, Heartbreak Hotel, Are You Lonesome Tonight?, That’s All Right, Jailhouse Rock, Blue Suede Shoes, Don’t Be Cruel and Love Me Tender.

programme.title: Chuck Berry in Concert
programme.desc: Legendary rock 'n' roller Chuck Berry performs at the BBC Television Theatre in 1972. Johnny B Goode, Roll Over Beethoven and Nadine are just some of the highlights of this concert, shown in an extended cut. This version includes, for the first time, an epic rendition of My Ding-a-Ling that carries all before it and raises innuendo to an art form.

programme.title: Tales of Rock 'n' Roll: Heartbreak Hotel
programme.desc: A look at Elvis Presley's first million-selling release, Heartbreak Hotel. This film tracks down the song's writers, schoolteacher Mae Axton and country musician Tommy Durden, and discovers that Heartbreak Hotel was inspired by a real-life suicide in Miami in 1955.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter, Jamie Theakston and Jayne Middlemiss present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 December 1999 and featuring DJ Jurgen Presents Alice Deejay, Cliff Richard, Martine McCutcheon, Robbie Williams and Steps and Westlife.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Mayo present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 June 1987, and featuring Samantha Fox, Whitesnake, Tom Jones, ABC, Chris Rea, Janet Jackson, Curiosity Killed the Cat, The Firm and Broken English.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 22 June 1990 and featuring Magnum, Roxette, Big Fun & Sonia, Wilson Phillips, Yazz, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Luciano Pavarotti and Elton John.

programme.title: Elvis Presley: '68 Comeback Special
programme.desc: In this remastered and extended concert recording, Elvis struts his stuff in his own charming and inimitable style, performing a massive collection of his hits, including Hound Dog, All Shook Up, Heartbreak Hotel, Are You Lonesome Tonight?, That’s All Right, Jailhouse Rock, Blue Suede Shoes, Don’t Be Cruel and Love Me Tender.

programme.title: This is BBC Four

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Nature documentary looking at a post office in Antarctica that is surrounded by 3,000 gentoo penguins.

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: In 1976, David Attenborough embarked on his most ambitious project ever, Life on Earth. No-one had ever attempted a natural history series on this scale before, and David’s reputation was on the line. This is the remarkable story behind the BBC’s most famous wildlife blockbuster: a three-year, hair-raising odyssey around the world, travelling to 40 countries and filming over 600 species, including the famous mountain gorillas. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenborough’s career as the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of our time.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: The first ever episode of the landmark natural history series Life on Earth. David Attenborough explores the wildlife and landscape that inspired Charles Darwin's theory of evolution.

programme.title: Goolagong
programme.desc: Evonne Goolagong's Wimbledon debut prompts memories of her rise from Barellan to tennis stardom, while she struggles with the controlling behaviour of her coach Vic Edwards.

programme.title: Goolagong
programme.desc: Evonne navigates controversy, pressure and the fight for equal pay, while personal loss and burnout drive her to walk away from tennis at the peak of her career.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend. The townsfolk do not look kindly on yet more intrusion into their still raw lives, but Scarsden begins to doubt the accepted version of events of that tragic day.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners. He discovers that Mandy Bond is the estranged daughter of Harley Reagan, but when he visits Reagan on his out-of-the-way estate, he comes across two sets of skeletal remains.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Count your blessings, they say. For some early retirement can be a blessing - for Hyacinth's Richard, this particular blessing comes very heavily disguised.

programme.title: Making Life on Earth: Attenborough's Greatest Adventure
programme.desc: In 1976, David Attenborough embarked on his most ambitious project ever, Life on Earth. No-one had ever attempted a natural history series on this scale before, and David’s reputation was on the line. This is the remarkable story behind the BBC’s most famous wildlife blockbuster: a three-year, hair-raising odyssey around the world, travelling to 40 countries and filming over 600 species, including the famous mountain gorillas. First broadcast in 1979 and watched by 500 million people, it launched David Attenborough’s career as the most successful and influential wildlife film-maker of our time.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: Not long ago, artificial intelligence was being sold as a science-fiction reality, a force that could transform society, cure disease, and remake the economy. Now, the mood is shifting. The companies driving the boom are racing for investment while grappling with a tougher reality: AI is costly, unpredictable, and not yet profitable. Even some of its creators warn progress may be too fast, as public anxiety grows and political resistance takes shape. Is AI now at a tipping point - caught between financial pressure, technological risk and a rising backlash?

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Shiona McCallum visits Fifa HQ in Zurich to see some of the latest tech being used in the World Cup. Martin Sharkey explores how changes in the way we watch content are shaping new technologies. Paul Carter hits the racetrack to see if AI technology systems can make journeys safer, and Andrew Rogers meets the dogs being helped to walk and run again thanks to assistive mobility aids.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Across the world, fashion is a fundamental expression of who we are. Today, people in China are rediscovering the beauty of their extraordinarily rich clothing heritage. In this programme, Alastair discovers how historic fashion design still resonates with people in China today, and how it has become a way in which people can celebrate their identity.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: World Questions
programme.desc: A special edition of World Questions, putting young Moroccan women at the centre of a national debate about rights, faith and family law reform. Jonny Dymond chairs a panel of feminist activists and conservatives as they debate issues such as child marriage, sex outside marriage, inheritance, harassment and polygamy. As a new generation pushes for change, Morocco stands out in the Muslim world, with reform being debated openly, and this programme hears from young women at a pivotal moment in civil society.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A global, topical weekly show that lifts the hood on the entertainment and news business. The Media Show explores the companies and personalities who command our attention, and how our perception of the world is shaped by their actions. From the streaming giants who entertain us, to the news outlets and social media platforms that inform and enrage us, The Media Show explains how the industry really works.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: Train enthusiast Francis Bourgeois travels to Malaysia to explore a railway network dating back 140 years. Jumping on board the iconic Jungle Railway, he experiences one of Southeast Asia's most spectacular train journeys, before arriving in the bustling capital city, Kuala Lumpur, and finishing up on Penang Island for their spectacular Lunar New Year celebrations. En route he joins forces with some of Malaysia's train devotees, finding a shared and nostalgic affection for the country's older, and less common freight trains.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This time, he’s joined by Stephen Mangan, Kimberley Walsh, Jonathan Ross, Craig Revel Horwood, Fatiha El-Ghorri and Nella Rose.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Uzbekistan were one of the first teams to secure their World Cup finals place a year ago, and they meet Colombia in their opening game in Group K in Mexico City. It’s a first ever World Cup appearance for Uzbekistan, who are managed by Italian former World Cup winner Fabio Cannavaro. Colombia were Copa America runners-up in 2024 and beat both Argentina and Brazil in qualifying for this tournament. The class and flair of Bayern Munich winger Luis Diaz means they could be a team to watch this summer.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets the doorbell detectives whose pet camera helped snare a cat burglar. Shocking video doorbell footage shows a masked gang on the rampage in the early hours of the morning. The theft of a doorbell camera is the downfall for one criminal as he returns to the scene of a crime only to discover the stolen doorbell has alerted the homeowner. And former police inspector Anton Sullivan is the cop turned robber as he burgles a home to help the rest of us stay one step ahead of the thieves.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A pair of brothers tackle their first project together in Stoke, and they're ready to roll up their sleeves to get stuck in. Martel is in Aberdeen to see a flat in need of a facelift. And in Suffolk, we get reacquainted with an old friend of the show who loves tackling a bungalow.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian heads to Lancashire, where experts Izzie Balmer and Colin Young help the teams find the best items to sell at auction. Danny also visits the British Commercial Vehicle Museum in Leyland to see several vehicles of special significance.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A suspicious package leads to the arrest of a man at the cruise ship terminal. Plus, a double bike collision involving two sets of tourists and a medical emergency at a street festival.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors take on scammers posing as the police, who even use fake recordings of walkie-talkie chatter to frighten their targets into believing they are genuinely speaking to the authorities. To avoid the suspicion of a very paranoid, aggressive scam group, the hub team send out the ground interceptors to avoid detection.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Suffolk, helping a couple find a new home in a more rural setting, where they can enjoy retired life together, closer to the coast, lakes or waterways. Also on the shopping list is a large, sociable kitchen, at least three bedrooms for hosting guests and a welcoming community close to hand.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill transform a plot that is just grass, two patios, a shed and a tree stump into Annie and Alex's dream garden, full of bees, birdsong and wildlife.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Czech Republic play their second game in Group A against South Africa at Atlanta Stadium. Since losing in the final for a second time in 1962 as Czechoslovakia, they have only once made it past the initial group stage – reaching the quarter-finals in 1990. This is their first World Cup in 20 years. They could rely on the experience of West Ham’s Tomas Soucek in midfield. Despite not getting out of the group stage in any of their three previous World Cups, the Bafana Bafana have only lost three of their nine matches, and famously beat holders France in 2010.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea receives an unexpected offer, Ian turns to Kathy for help, and Bea meddles with Honey and Billy.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police launch high-risk searches for two missing women - 22-year-old Santra was last seen on CCTV 10 days ago, while 52-year-old Laura vanished without trace two weeks ago.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting fact from fiction this week are guests Jo Brand, Sam Quek, Bobby Seagull and Joe Thomas.

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as three parliamentary constituencies choose a new MP. The BBC's political editor, Chris Mason, is at the count in Makerfield, where the mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham, is seeking a return to Westminster. Scotland correspondents are live in Aberdeen and Arbroath, as the SNP tries to hold two marginal seats that it won at the 2024 General Election. Political correspondent Alex Forsyth is in the studio, keeping her sharp eye across the latest political reaction. Professor Sir John Curtice analyses the key electoral trends. And a panel of senior politicians give their reactions to the verdicts.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets the husband and wife doorbell detectives who used their doorbell video to follow the clue from a criminal’s tattoo and get the men who robbed their neighbour put behind bars. More footage shows how two masked men trapped a couple in their home just to steal the catalytic converter from a prized vehicle. And former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows the one simple way you can prevent your car being broken into.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Featuring a Kent house with not one, but two front doors. Will it be twice as much work or double the profit? Plus, two fascinating Welsh auction lots.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In Devon, Christina Trevanion learns the incredible story of the World War II soldier who bravely played the bagpipes as bullets rained down on British commandos during the D-Day landings in Normandy. Bill Millin’s son John explains how his Scottish father was ordered to play his pipes as troops landed on Sword Beach on 6 June 1944 – and how fearless Bill unwittingly mooned German troops in the process! A battle of a very different kind is taking place in Exeter among the antiques stalls as two teams of friends go bargain-hunting with experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe. Long-time pals Sue and Jane have animal collectibles on their shopping list, while mates Ben and Kieran are on a mission to make the biggest profit on the show ever. As the hour ticks down, the blue team make Gary laugh when they mistake a Victorian washing dolly for a toilet plunger. Over with the reds, Ben is lost for words when he spots a tantalus, and tension rises at the auction when the winning team is announced.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: At a wine conference, Tippy is in the spotlight, Louis learns of Philippe’s past, and Daisy makes a drunken move.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton relishes being in his home county of West Sussex as he shows off some of its many delights including, of course, a selection of beautiful properties. He is with a married couple who want to follow in his footsteps by escaping London in search of a new life and home in the countryside.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Frances Tophill are in Leicester to help Pritesh and Mita transform their run-down plot, which is cluttered with discarded furniture, uneven patio areas and struggling plants, into a garden inspired by India, with blooming marigolds and homegrown vegetables. Pritesh and Mita dream of a patio and pergola for meals and entertaining their extended family, a water feature and a cosy seating area. They are also hoping for raised beds to revive their vegetable patch and flowers, a cherished project for the space that used to be looked after by their late grandmother. With Mita’s health declining and daily life demanding their attention, the garden has fallen into disrepair. Now, they hope to restore it as a beautiful, vibrant space that honours their grandmother’s memory and brings the family together. Can the Garden Rescue team turn this neglected plot into a flourishing Indian-inspired garden on a £5,500 budget?

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Martin from Morecambe has always been obsessed with the world of entertainment. Well, his uncle was a lion tamer after all! So, after sharing the bill with greats like Ken Dodd and Danny La Rue, he was bitten by the performing bug and settled in the showbiz capital of Blackpool. An avid collector with a penchant for puppets, Martin’s home has gradually been taken over by a whole host of circus-style paraphernalia, and it’s time to evict some. Izzie Balmer and Robin Johnson are on hand to clear some space and raise some funds so that Martin can fulfil his dream of launching a puppet museum. Izzie has just one day to work her way through Martin’s eclectic bonkers collection, while Robin will be adding extra value restoring those items in need of a bit of TLC, from a ventriloquist puppet who has lost his strings to an out-of-key musical machine. There’s no denying today’s challenges are unique.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as host nation USA face Australia at the Seattle Stadium in their second game in Group D. Coached by former Tottenham boss Mauricio Pochettino, the USA are making a twelfth appearance at the tournament, with their best result being a semi-final appearance back in the very first World Cup in 1930. Australia reached the last 16 in Qatar four years ago before being knocked out by the eventual winners Argentina. These two teams have never met before at the World Cup.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Bruce gets an offer that's hard to refuse, Serena finds out how much everyone loves a baby, and Matt helps Ron log on to his computer.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: The birthing centre is finally ready to open, and Alex is helping Joyce hire a head midwife, but Joyce has a problem with one of the applicants. Bruce joins Matt for his first ambulance ride-along. Serena and Ron butt heads over the emergency ward's best pen.

programme.title: Road House
programme.desc: Ex-UFC fighter Dalton is persuaded to take a job as a bouncer at a Florida Keys road house, only to learn that the bar is being terrorised by a gang working for a local crime boss.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Julie Lin and Peter McKenna, and by special guest Lenny Henry. Drinks expert Olly Smith picks the wines to go with the studio dishes, and there are some great moments from the BBC food archive.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: When he's not touring the world, comedian Ed Byrne loves nothing more than to escape the bright lights for the splendour of the Wicklow mountains, Ireland's largest national park. Ed and Anna hike the Sally Gap, and he surprises her with a meal on the mountainside. After their feast, they search for sika deer in Glendalough with a local park ranger, learning about deer management. Anna also visits a sheep farming family known for their award-winning halloumi-style cheese.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp heads to Kingston upon Thames for this episode. Two teams battle it out at the Kingston Antiques Centre with experts Charles Hanson and Thomas Forrester on hand to help our teams bag a bargain, but who can buy the most profitable three items to take to auction? Natasha also meets a local artist and learns about a photographic technique that is over 150 years old, before trying it out herself.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
programme.desc: Live coverage of the men's singles semi-finals at the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as three-time World Cup finalists Netherlands stay in Texas for their second Group F game, this time in Houston against Sweden. Netherlands were unbeaten in qualifying, scoring 27 goals in eight matches, with Memphis Depay bagging eight. Sweden earned their finals place by beating Poland in the play-offs after missing out on the World Cup four years ago. Both the previous meetings between these sides at major tournaments finished 0-0, with the only World Cup encounter back in 1974.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Stevie faces the consequences of her actions.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: The group are trapped indoors by a heatwave and Jess determines to get her family on the next boat home. Tensions mount as the power shorts, the stolen money resurfaces and unwanted visitors threaten to destroy Zoe’s chance at happiness with Solomon. Can she escape what the Fates seem to have in store for her? Nat and Jacob have some making up to do but as the temperature soars, they too are pushed to breaking point. This isn’t going to end well.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The gang belatedly get involved in the Great Famine, the one thing they didn’t need to get involved in. Callum’s new job at the Ballymac Folk Museum sends him down a spiral of method acting as a starving peasant. And he’s helped along in this grovelling subservience by his new boss, tyrannical land steward Phillip. The sudden news, to Jordan, that a million people died during the potato famine causes him to finally develop empathy, and it turns out to be a real pain.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Strange things are afoot in Ballymacnoose: animals in small clothing, paranormal sightings of leprechauns, and most bizarrely of all, Callum gets his first ever match on a dating app. The one prickle on the pickle is that Gwen, Callum’s dream girl, is also in an open relationship with the 50-year-old Grainne, a woman who disembowels chickens for a living. Meanwhile, Gemma becomes obsessed with dressing farm animals in tiny clothing and causes Sammy to spin out in paranoia thinking that his farm has become infested with leprechauns. So Lorcan has no other choice but to introduce Gemma to the dark secrets of the Ballymacnoose Paranormal Society, also run by his da and Frank.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Life in Ballymacnoose becomes very complicated when Callum accidentally walks in on Gemma and Lorcan trying out a new form of lovemaking. The sight triggers a long-buried childhood trauma in Callum and raises uncomfortable questions about the town’s deeply repressed relationship with sex. Can Ballymacnoose open its heart to true love? Meanwhile, Jordan has officially begun dating his much older lover, 50-year-old Grainne. But as their relationship deepens, Jordan is confronted with another harsh reality of adulthood: he’s going bald. Faced with the slow retreat of his hairline and the love of a good woman, Jordan must decide whether to cling to denial or embrace manhood in all its forms - love, responsibility and mushroom carbonaras.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: As Callum begins slowly unravelling under the strain of Jordan's relentless night terrors about baldness, he stumbles upon a flyer for Ballymacbot Wars - a local robot combat tournament offering a generous cash prize and potential ticket out of Jordan’s bedroom. Meanwhile, Jordan is uncharacteristically uninterested in the whole affair. He's become a bald hermit, terrified to leave the house and expose his creamy scalp to the outside world. That is, until, like a dagger in the night, a hooded burst of inspiration sends him back out into the world… and the robot wars.

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: A special cricket edition of the general knowledge quiz in which four teams try to come up with the answers that no-one else could think of. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and co-host Richard Osman, with contestants Alison Mitchell, Andy Zaltzman, Devon Malcolm, Graham Gooch, Georgie Heath, Fran Wilson, Graeme Swann and Mark Ramprakash.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as World Cup debutants Curacao play their second match in Group E against Ecuador at Kansas City Stadium. Curacao are the smallest nation ever to qualify for a World Cup and have the oldest coach in the tournament's history: 78-year-old former Rangers and Sunderland manager Dick Advocaat. Ecuador impressively finished second in South American qualifying and boast Chelsea midfielder Moises Caicedo in their ranks. It’s 20 years since their best performance at a World Cup, making the last 16 in 2006, when they were knocked out by England.

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy starts at the new gym, but he is soon spooked by high-energy fitness influencer Kara’s talk of high standards. Will he be fired in his first week? And will his long-distance bromance with Spooner survive?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: It’s mental health week at the gym, and Andy is worried that his relationship with Georgia is drifting. Will her suggestion of a threesome fix things? Or will Andy ruin everything?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy is dumped and embarks on a brutal ‘revenge body’ diet. Spooner and Blue hold a wild house party to reclaim their cool. Will Andy’s nerve hold? Or will we see the return of the legendary 'Mad' Andy and his firework?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy is (sort of) diagnosed with low testosterone and is in a funk. Kara endorses a pre-workout that has unfortunate side effects. Jonathan and Eunice start a steamy affair. Can Andy get his manly mojo back?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Kara offers Andy the chance to be a fitness influencer. Free stuff! He and Spooner make up, but Kara has other ideas for Andy, including a swaggy makeover. Jonathan is banned from the gym, as Eunice can’t resist his charms.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all – until someone else turns up with the same idea. Awkward.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads out to find one of the UK’s greatest predators: the dragonfly. He witnesses the aerial prowess of the hobby and dives underwater with the puffin.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of bat-hunting boas, meets a team working to save the deadly but cute slow loris, and he comes face to face with the king of snakes: the king cobra.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine show with Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond. This week's show is an American special as Jeremy, Richard and James try to prove that a fly-drive holiday to Florida would be more fun if you bought a car instead of renting one. Having picked up some wheels for no more than 1000 dollars each, the boys embark on an epic road trip to find out who got the best deal, encountering heat, flies and very angry locals along the way.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: An all-star cast arrives in Scotland under the cover of darkness for the ultimate game of deception and the chance to win up to $250,000. The cards of fate are dealt, secrets lurk in the shadows, and the game starts faster than ever before.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: Secrets surround the castle. The first Faithful is murdered, the mission causes friction, and some overwhelming evidence is brought to the Round Table. The pressure is on as the Traitors must commit their next murder right under the Faithfuls' noses.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: The Faithful are given an early chance to avenge the Traitors' latest murder. The stakes are higher than ever in the mission, as the players must put their life in the hands of the person they trust the most. A brutal end awaits one Faithful.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: A fiery mission results in ten players up for murder. One player has some damning evidence, while another lets their suspicions be known by the wrong person. There is a big swing at the Round Table, and the Traitors feel the weight of their decisions.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Stephanie, a small-town vlogger and single mother, tries to uncover the truth behind her best friend Emily's sudden disappearance. She is joined by Emily's husband Sean on a thrilling investigation filled with twists and betrayals, secrets and revelations, love and loyalty, murder and revenge.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Stay-at-home-daughter Chi is suddenly forced to fend for herself when her parents die in a car crash. Chi learns she will only inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left in the parents' will, the first of which is, ironically, learn to drive. Meanwhile, Chi's highly strung auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her dependent pest of a niece, but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Chi ventures out into Ramsgate in search of a driving license and infiltrates the family of a mild-mannered driving instructor in the process.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassess his life and values.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes searching for Patagonia’s perfect predator: the puma. After waiting his whole life to see one, Steve sees a tiny cub and witnesses a female puma hunting an armadillo.

programme.title: Shrek Forever After
programme.desc: Rumpelstiltskin fools Shrek, who is suffering from a midlife crisis, into being erased from existence and placed in a sinister alternative reality ruled by Rumpel. Can Shrek restore his old life? The solution is just a kiss away... Animated comedy fantasy adventure.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Friendship, loyalty, laughter and lots of mischief bring best pals Conor MacSweeney and Jock O’Keeffe into ongoing conflict with their school headmaster, Principal Barry Walsh. Getting caught up in the chaos is Conor's long suffering mother Mairead, something that happens very often as she tries to pick up the pieces after the lads' misbehaviour causes problems. A not-so-clever plan to earn some extra cash backfires, bringing the lads head to head with Sergeant Healy, who has been hot on their heels for some time and is determined to catch them up to no good.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy following the coming-of-age adventures of two best friends. Conor and Jock's friendships with the principal's daughters intensify, leading to a comic showdown.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The lads persuade the girls to spend a full day with them on a beautiful adventure.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and his mam Mairead’s much loved fridge, bought by Conor’s dad, has finally given up on them. Remembering when the fridge was originally bought in town miles away, Mairead decides that a family bonding session on a road trip to that same town to buy a replacement fridge will be just the thing to bond with her son.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock have been instructed to take up unpaid part-time work experience at Mairead's side on the fish stall in the English Market, bringing them up close and personal with life in the real world. With Jock's extra mouth to feed in the McSweeney household, money is getting more and more tight, leaving the trio to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help out Mairead financially, the lads start to think about ways they can come up with some extra cash. The freak local catch of a very expensive fish gives the two lads an idea, leading them on a dubious adventure involving breaking and entering, a hilarious struggle and a memorable trip through Cork with their ill-gotten gains. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level, while Mairead tries to give Healy some driving lessons.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Facing a dilemma, Siobhan makes a plan with Linda to meet the lads on a bus. An unexpected intervention by local bully Billy Murphy leads to an accidental wild bus ride around Cork, ultimately bringing everyone closer together.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. His girlfriend moves out when a rat appears in his flat, then menacing rival dealer Psycho Paul has a score to settle.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. While his girlfriend is away, Moz tries to seduce the beautiful China, but his plans are interrupted by another appearance from the rat.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy series starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz learns about a local murder, meets his stalker and makes up with Nicki. Meanwhile, China's new boyfriend Cartoon Head has something to hide.

programme.title: Ideal
programme.desc: Manchester-based comedy starring Johnny Vegas as small-time dope dealer Moz. Moz gives a local band a hand and the police help him shake off his stalker. After China splits with Cartoon Head, Moz is keen to provide a shoulder to cry on.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: It’s the first day of sixth form, and Mike is looking forward to a bit of social mobility. He thinks he’s way better than the group of so-called 'loser' friends he’s somehow accrued, and aspires to higher things. But for now, he’s stuck with powergeek Sonny and Dan, a lad who only communicates in put-downs, and he’s going to find it hard to shake them off: Mike, Sonny and Dan all take the mobility bus to school. Mike has cerebral palsy, Sonny uses a wheelchair, and Dan ‘Syndrome’ Simmons has Down’s Syndrome. So Mike, Sonny and Dan are trapped together – at least for the ride to school.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Doctor and Clara face their last Christmas. Trapped on an Arctic base, under attack from terrifying creatures, who are you going to call? Santa Claus!

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda has decided to prove to herself and her friends that she can officially be an adult. She starts by looking after a toddler, but the soft play centre is too much of a lure and she gets stuck in a kids' tunnel. She then hosts a dinner party for eight, which ends in inevitable chaos.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda has been caring for her ill mother for five very long days. Trapped in her flat and being barked at by Penny, she is forced to think about her life regrets. Before she has the chance to tick off her bucket list she gets ill, and Tilly, Gary and Stevie offer no sympathy or help at all.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: The Rose family's lives are changed forever when the government comes to collect years of back taxes. Left with almost nothing, they must move to Johnny’s last remaining asset, Schitt’s Creek. When Johnny offends the town’s mayor, Roland Schitt, the Rose family soon discovers that the doors to their motel rooms have been taken. It’s now up to Johnny to set things right in the hope that his family can sleep safely on their first night in their new home.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: After Johnny awakes soaking wet from a leaking pipe in the ceiling, he decides that he must sell the town, but soon discovers that this is easier said than done. He needs to get Roland’s approval before he can put the town on the market. Meanwhile, the Rose children check out the local nightlife by attending a tailgate party. Alexis uses the opportunity to scope for a new boyfriend, while David gets to know Stevie, the girl that runs the motel.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny is shocked to see the pornographic-looking town sign and plans to get it taken down. When he talks to the town council about it, he finds out that the sign is very important to Roland for family reasons. Meanwhile, David is faced with the near-impossible task of finding a job, while Alexis starts her community service and is paired with Mutt, the guy she kissed the night before.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny and Moira come to a shocking realisation that they might be terrible parents. They attempt to get to know their twenty-something children, but David and Alexis avoid their parents' intrusive interest, with David trying to sell some of his clothes for extra cash and Alexis investigating Mutt’s mysterious relationship with the mayor’s wife. Johnny and Moira speak with Roland and Jocelyn about parenting and realise that all parents have issues with their children.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown has family troubles to contend with: her son Dermot has fallen out with Maria Nicholson, his long-term girlfriend, and Grandad is feeling poorly. After a sleepless night, Mrs Brown decides to try to sort out Dermot and Maria's problem by giving Maria the benefit of her advice. But her heart-to-heart with Maria only succeeds in making the dispute worse. So Mrs Brown's daughter Cathy intervenes. She's a psychology student, and offers Dermot and Maria a counselling session. A dismayed Mrs Brown can't help interfering, and the session quickly descends into a blazing row between her and Cathy... with an effect on Dermot and Maria that not even Agnes Brown could have predicted. Meanwhile, Grandad ends up in hospital when Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes dramatically wrong. And Cathy reveals the electrifying secret of how she keeps dodgy men at bay.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown is concerned that her family are keeping too many secrets. Her daughter Cathy won't let her meet her new boyfriend. Her elder son Mark won't reveal why his feisty wife Betty has thrown him out. And her younger son Dermot has lied to his fiancee Maria Nicholson's well-to-do parents about the status of his job. When Betty and Mrs Brown meet to discuss Mark's problem, they unsurprisingly come to blows. Unfortunately their row happens in front of Dermot's posh future mother-in-law Mrs Nicholson, who has come round to discuss arrangements for her daughter's wedding. Horrified by what she witnesses, Mrs Nicholson decides that the Brown family are so rough she will try to stop them attending the wedding. Then Mrs Brown spots Betty with another man, and concludes that Betty and Mark have fallen out because Betty is having an affair. But when she finally confronts Betty, Agnes is confounded to discover that Mark is the one who has something to hide: a guilty secret for which Agnes has only herself to blame.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Janet is keen to improve her mind but realises that neither poetry nor biscuits are the answer. Gaz is desperate to feel attractive and thinks that the best way to accomplish this is to flash his rather underdeveloped chest.
