programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Acting on a tip off, the Peelers storm a city centre flat on a drugs bust. Inside, a startled suspect is immediately cuffed and arrested while outside, the PSNI team search the nearby streets for the suspected big-time dealer they think may have fled the scene with more drugs. On Great Victoria Street in Belfast city centre, police attempt to arrest a man who refuses to comply with a search. The situation quickly escalates, and he is detained by a number of officers who use a spit guard and leg restraints. The man is taken into custody, where he continues to kick off. Officers and custody staff surround the man in his cell as they try to get him under control. Later, Stephen interviews the deputy chief constable, Bobby Singleton, and questions him about some PSNI tactics and officers’ use of force.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Accusations fly when the housekeeper reveals she saw Jon desecrating her local chapel with ‘one of them’ last night. On a group outing to an ancient temple, Zoe, Solomon and Jacob are caught in a terrible car accident that threatens to ruin not just the holiday but also their lives. As Solomon admits his failings to Zoe, Jacob starts to suspect she is the one to blame for all this. Nat desperately tries to undo a drunken mistake, leaving Jess and Dan alone together for the first time since the kiss.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Suffolk with a couple from an East Sussex town who are utterly devoted to their three whippets and are desperate to leave their 1960s bungalow. They dream of running a small glamping escape, complete with a shepherd’s hut for guests and a garden for their speedy, four-legged companions. Property picks include a striking mystery house and even homes with more than one staircase! Steve also takes to his bicycle to test out a newly created cycling route that winds through some of Suffolk’s most glorious countryside.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and JJ go rummaging in Woking, helping a grieving widow sort through years of zoo memorabilia. Can gold sovereigns and a stone eagle fund a bedroom for Jan’s great-niece?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As summer hits its stride at Longmeadow, Monty Don brings a burst of colour to the Jewel Garden with vibrant annuals, plants out tender veg, and shares some practical advice for tackling greenhouse pests. Arit Anderson visits a striking garden in Greater Manchester packed with clever design solutions and wildlife-friendly planting that comes alive by day and night. In Dorset, Joe Swift explores a much-loved family garden reimagined for three generations. In Essex, a gardener has transformed a shaded, north-facing plot into a lush, lawn-free haven, while in Bristol, a Morris dancer channels movement and rhythm into her planting as she prepares for Gardeners’ World Live in Birmingham.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 11 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ben Bailey Smith, Robin Ince, Lara Ricote and Helen Skelton take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s Scandinavian rail adventure reaches Denmark’s second city, Aarhus, an architectural playground with dazzling contemporary buildings set alongside historic half-timbered houses. At the striking mid-century city hall, he learns about its design and one of its architects, Arne Jacobsen. At Jelling, Michael reads the runes to discover the birthplace of Denmark. Once a royal Viking burial site, Jelling has two 10th-century runic stones. Michael connects with King Harald Bluetooth to learn how Norse paganism gave way to Christianity. Michael crosses the Old Little Belt Bridge from Jutland to Middelfart on the island of Funen, once a whaling centre and now home to a large population of the smallest whales, porpoises. At Odense, he is transported back to the time of Danish author Hans Christian Andersen at a living history village, where he struggles to walk in clogs and learns to make bread.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 2 highlights from the second Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: In Hampshire, archaeologist Ben Robinson investigates how a tiny village, originally meant to be a free port to process sugar, became a bustling naval shipyard. Ben learns that three ships built here would lead Nelson to victory at Trafalgar. Then, in an almost untouched 18th-century labourer’s cottage, Ben gets a feel for just how basic life was for hardworking rural families 250 years ago.

programme.title: Queen James: Passion, Plots and Power in the Court of Britain’s First King
programme.desc: Gareth Russell reveals the story of King James VI and I, his fondness for male favourites and a scandalous murder trial that threatened to blow his monarchy apart.

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: You could be a scroll away from finding a sperm donor. But at what cost? With fertility prices soaring and treatment difficult to access for some, same-sex female couples say they have little choice but to turn to social media to make a baby. Social media groups and apps are fuelling a cheap, easy and totally unregulated donor market. But it’s risky: outside of a sperm clinic, there’s no guarantee of health screening, and you’re not legally protected. And it’s not just a gamble for those wanting to get pregnant. According to the UK’s fertility regulator, men who donate sperm outside of clinics may also be breaking the law if they’re storing, processing or sending sperm. But what do they think of this? Meet the men travelling the world to donate sperm, and find out what happens when reporter Gemma Dunstan tries to order a sample online.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O’Connell.

programme.title: Gods of Tennis
programme.desc: It’s 1975, and two tennis greats – Billie Jean King and Arthur Ashe – are aiming to win Wimbledon titles but also, in their own ways, to change the world for the better. Back in the late 60s, as the women’s liberation movement becomes headline news, Billie Jean King takes up the gauntlet for women in sport. Having dominated the women's game for years, winning several Wimbledon Championships, King is spearheading the drive for equal prize money and equal treatment of women, whilst Ashe focuses his attention on racial equality on and off the court.

programme.title: The Phantom of the Open
programme.desc: The heartwarming true story of shipyard crane operator Maurice Flitcroft, who, having never played a round of golf in his life, entered the 1976 British Open and subsequently shot the worst round of golf in Open history, drawing the ire of the golfing elite but becoming a folk hero in the process.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Mexico play their second game in Group A against South Korea in Guadalajara Stadium. This is Mexico’s ninth consecutive appearance and a record third overall as hosts or co-hosts; they reached the quarter-finals on each of the previous two occasions, in 1970 and 1986. Mexico hold the record, at 60, for the most World Cup matches without ever winning the trophy. South Korea are the only Asian team to reach the semi-finals, as co-hosts in 2002. This is their 11th consecutive World Cup and 12th overall, the most of any Asian nation. They are due to be captained by former Tottenham forward Son Heung-Min.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in the East Riding of Yorkshire with a couple heading home after a life in France, along with their dog Daisy and a serious craving for family, friends and proper fish and chips. With £280,000 to spend, they explore a tempting mix of characterful older homes and smart modern properties. Along the way, Alistair also learns about a local scheme helping to create affordable housing so local people can continue to put down roots in this much-loved corner of the county.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill transform a plot that is just grass, two patios, a shed and a tree stump into Annie and Alex's dream garden, full of bees, birdsong and wildlife.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From the conservation of an apprentice indenture from 1785 that tied a 13-year-old boy to a cotton mill, to the unusual homes of working families at Kinver Edge carved into a sandstone hillside, this episode uncovers the hidden lives of ordinary working people – and follows the teams making sure their stories are never forgotten.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 12 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ben Bailey Smith, Robin Ince, Lara Ricote and Helen Skelton take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo concludes his epic rail journey across southern Norway, the Jutland Peninsula and the Danish archipelago. He crosses one of Europe’s epic rail engineering projects: the Great Belt Bridge, an 18-kilometre transport link stretching between the islands of Funen and Zealand, composed of a box girder bridge, a tunnel and a suspension bridge. At the bottom of Roskilde Fjord, he alights in the city of Roskilde, once a Viking trading hub. Five 1,000-year-old Viking vessels are preserved here, and to study their construction, seaworthy replicas are built. Michael is excited to set sail in one. In Copenhagen, Michael chills out in Europe’s cosiest capital in pursuit of the Danish concept of 'hygge'. What does it mean? Michael consults the experts. His next stop is Allerod, where Danish furniture design broke through in the mid-20th century. Icons such as the Papa Bear chair and the Round Chair are still made here. Finally, on the north eastern tip of Zealand, Michael reaches the magnificent 16th-century Kronborg Castle, the setting for Shakespeare’s Hamlet.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 3 highlights from the second Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A special programme from Birmingham in the heart of Gardeners’ World Live – an annual vibrant celebration of practical gardening and bold design. Monty Don is joined by Frances Tophill, Adam Frost, Arit Anderson and Sue Kent for an hour packed with ideas, inspiration and expert advice. The team explore standout gardens, meet the people shaping the future of horticulture and reveal practical takeaways viewers can try at home. Sustainability is in the spotlight as pioneering growers reveal why cork could be a game-changing material – and how it’s already transforming the way they garden. There’s a surprising and ambitious show garden co-designed by Professor Alice Roberts, bringing the story of evolution to life through plants, and a first-time designer whose passion for railways has inspired a unique garden guaranteed to turn heads.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: Hidden behind two famous gardens lie the legacies of two extraordinary designers: garden designer Gertrude Jekyll at Munstead Wood, one of the National Trust's newest properties, and flamboyant set designer Oliver Messel at Nymans, which overlooks the Sussex Weald. Together, they feature secret laboratories and historic seedlings, topiary cats and exquisite portraits.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Everyone says the Season 7 finale of fantasy epic The Ninth Circle was pretty disappointing; even showrunner Spencer Maguire is keen to move on to new projects. But not everyone thinks that it has to be the end of the matter. In fact, Ninth Circle obsessive Simon Smethurst decides to visit his hero to help put things right. Because that’s what the fans would want…

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: TRNSMT
programme.desc: Shereen Cutkelvin presents highlights from the first day of TRNSMT live from Glasgow Green including Becky Hill, Dylan John Thomas, Cammy Barnes, Beluga Lagoon, Luvcat, The Beta Band and Wolf Alice.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police concerns grow about the mental state of two missing teens - 16-year-old Larissa has disappeared after an argument with her mum, and 18-year-old Bobby may be a suicide risk.

programme.title: Gareth Southgate: Changing the Game for Young Men
programme.desc: Former England men's football manager Gareth Southgate reveals the struggles many boys and young men face, asking why some feel left behind and what could help them move forward.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: In May last year, three arson attacks targeted the prime minister, Keir Starmer. Two properties and a car were set alight. But who lit the fires and why? Daniel De Simone investigates and finds the shadowy figures behind the attacks were also behind fake far-right groups trying to stoke hatred and division in the UK. The fires fit a pattern in which hostile states such as Russia are increasingly using proxies here to do their dirty work. So could Russia be behind the Starmer fires?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Out in the ocean, the Piripenguins encounter a massive, angry whale called Norman, who wrecks their boat. Can the Piripenguins brave their fears and find a way to cheer him up?

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Dad needs some training to pass his astronaut fitness test. Cosima and Big Lizard put him through his paces! When Rex gets into trouble, Dad’s hard work finally pays off.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The ducklings look after the sheep while wearing cowboy hats and riding a hen. This gives Weasy the idea to have a rodeo session with one of the sheep - but it won't buck! The ducklings must stop their father from upsetting the animals.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: While questing to keep her hero safe on her long journey, superfan Blossom fusses and frets too much, making things awkward.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: It's time for the racket game, where big scores mean big prizes.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When looking after their neighbours' baby, Duck soon learns that he is a terrible babysitter - though it seems kids love Frog! Duck needs Frog to help out for once but soon loses the baby.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Liko, Roy and Ult reunite with Dot and Murdock in Cortondo.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Adam and Joe race across Lapland to deliver Callum’s letter to Santa before putting their elf skills to the test in the ultimate Christmas challenge!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro, Fire, Hammer and Apollo go head-to-head to rack up prank points - but will ponytails, air guitars, inflatable flamingos or zebras lead to a tap-out?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Hacker meets the stars of Toy Story 5, while Abby takes two super fans behind the scenes of West End production My Neighbour Totoro, with exclusive access to the magical puppets which bring the story to life. Also, Queen of the Jungle Scarlett Moffatt joins us in the studio!

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A special programme from Birmingham in the heart of Gardeners’ World Live – an annual vibrant celebration of practical gardening and bold design. Monty Don is joined by Frances Tophill, Adam Frost, Arit Anderson and Sue Kent for an hour packed with ideas, inspiration and expert advice. The team explore standout gardens, meet the people shaping the future of horticulture and reveal practical takeaways viewers can try at home. Sustainability is in the spotlight as pioneering growers reveal why cork could be a game-changing material – and how it’s already transforming the way they garden. There’s a surprising and ambitious show garden co-designed by Professor Alice Roberts, bringing the story of evolution to life through plants, and a first-time designer whose passion for railways has inspired a unique garden guaranteed to turn heads.

programme.title: My Darling Clementine
programme.desc: Marshal Wyatt Earp tries to clean up the lawless town of Tombstone with the help of his brothers and gambler Doc Holliday. But trouble with the Clanton Gang leads to the legendary 'gunfight at the OK Corral'.

programme.title: She Wore a Yellow Ribbon
programme.desc: In the second in John Ford's cavalry trilogy, a US cavalry officer suffers a setback on his last mission and is 'retired' before he can take further action. To avert a full-scale war, he decides to act alone.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel heads to Shildon in County Durham to see a two-bed mid-terrace bought by an enthusiastic first-time developer. Dion visits a rather unusual lot in Derby that has great potential for transformation. Jacqui is in Seaford, East Sussex, to see a former care home that is also a great opportunity for a developer with the right skills.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: It’s all go in gorgeous Powys as Jules Hudson helps a couple to swap town life in Oxfordshire for his beloved Wales. On the wish list is space for two dogs, three cats and a garage for a motorbike, which the brand spanking new mystery eco-house might just deliver. For an inspiring change of pace, Jules visits a brilliant local farming initiative that’s helping young people get into agriculture and grow sustainable food for the future.

programme.title: Battle of Britain
programme.desc: 1940. Britain fears an impending German invasion. Goering is confident his Luftwaffe will pave the way for success. It falls to brave squadrons of fighter pilots to fend off the attack from the air.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin revisits some of the locations visited by Flog It! and presents all-new valuations. Experts touch down at Bletchley Park, the Ulster Transport Museum and Crowcombe Court to track down some unusual items to sell at auction. They uncover a beautiful watercolour and some stunning porcelain, and Paul Martin visits Antony in Cornwall to unearth a fascinating family history.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Two top TV journalists, former BBC royal correspondent Jennie Bond and newsreader Simon McCoy, take up the antiques challenge and go rummaging all around Devon. The two, who became firm friends whilst reporting on some quite tumultuous times, now find themselves chasing scoops once again. With £400 each and experts Margie Cooper and Raj Bisram along to help, Jennie and Simon will be piloting through the B-roads of Devon in an Austin Healey and an E-Type Jaguar.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton’s loch-hopping journey across Scotland reaches the quiet lochs of the Borders, where he learns dog-handling techniques at a school for sheepdogs and then heads to some man-made lochs in the Moorfoot Hills, where he hears tell of hidden treasure. In Edinburgh, he discovers the story of a Dutch-speaking Scottish minister on skates and goes in search of a lost river and two long-vanished lochs in the heart of the city. Crossing the Firth of Forth, Paul travels around the industrial lochs of the old Fife coal field, before walking in the footsteps of Mary, Queen of Scots on a haunted island in Loch Leven.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 4 highlights from the second Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: Famous names reflect on making some of TV's most popular and influential programmes.

programme.title: The Prince's Trust Rock Gala 1986
programme.desc: On 20 June 1986, to celebrate the tenth anniversary of the Prince's Trust, a concert of superstars from the world of pop music performed for the Prince and Princess of Wales. The birthday concert took place at Wembley Arena in London and featured Big Country, Level 42, Suzanne Vega and, for the one night only, the supergroup Birthday Party, including Phil Collins, Midge Ure, Mark Knopfler, Rod Stewart, Elton John, Eric Clapton, Joan Armatrading, Howard Jones, Paul Young, Tina Turner and special guests.

programme.title: Tina Turner: Wildest Dreams Live in Amsterdam 1996
programme.desc: Tina Turner lifted the roof off the new Amsterdam Arena for three sizzling nights in September 1996, in front of 150,000 people, as part of her record-breaking Wildest Dreams European tour, on which she performed over 150 shows to over 3,000,000 people. Performing many of the hits which have made her famous, this concert captures the spirit of those nights. Songs include River Deep Mountain High, Nutbush City Limits, GoldenEye, The Best, Steamy Windows, Missing You, In Your Wildest Dreams, We Don’t Need Another Hero, Private Dancer and What’s Love Got to Do with It.

programme.title: TRNSMT

programme.title: The Next Step
programme.desc: After months out of the studio, Nick announces that the nationals are back on - but which team is going? A-troupe and B-troupe must face off with five one-on-one matches to find out. However, a lot has changed, and things aren’t the way they used to be. At the end of the day, there is only room for one team to continue on the road to the nationals, and Richelle and Summer each make a choice that changes everything.

programme.desc: Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.[S]

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Matt Allwright
programme.desc: Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.[S]

programme.title: Vanessa
programme.desc: Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip![S]

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Beckett has her faith tested when she investigates the murder of an estate agent and all of the evidence points to Castle. (S5 Ep5) [S]

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Beckett and Castle teleport in to a sci-fi convention to investigate the murder of the fan of a cancelled TV show. (S5 Ep6)[S]

programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: In Brighton we meet growers at an allotment who offer their produce to local food banks. The charity Nurture through Nature is staffed by volunteers, for whom gardening is a release. (S1 Ep43)[S]

programme.title: 5 News with Dan Walker
programme.desc: Join Dan Walker and the team for the news that matters to you.[S]

programme.title: Isle of Wight: Jewel of the South
programme.desc: The seaside town of Sandown gears up for its carnival. Keen for new custom, cafe owner Vivette plans a barbecue to give revellers a taste of authentic Caribbean cooking. (S2 Ep4/4)[S]

programme.title: Croatia: Jewel of the...
programme.desc: ...Mediterranean. Exploring the sunniest island in Croatia, from secluded bays with beautiful beaches to glamorous hangouts. Plus, a look at how Hvar became the birthplace of organised tourism.[S]

programme.title: Draining Loch Ness
programme.desc: The plug is pulled on the famous loch, using comparisons with the deepest lake on earth, combined with military-grade scanning tech and a sonar curtain across its surface.[S]

programme.title: Milan with Michael Portillo
programme.desc: In Italy's capital of fashion, design and industry, Michael races fast cars, see the sites from the back of a 1960s Lambretta and learns the skills of Italian coffee making.[S]

programme.title: New: Piers Morgan: Uncensored
programme.desc: The bold, opinion-led news and debate show hosted by Piers Morgan. Piers blends journalism, debates and cultural commentary that's unapologetically direct.

programme.title: Murder: Suspect No.1
programme.desc: A man is brutally beaten to death in a Lancashire basement squat in the early hours. With the only suspect denying any involvement, can detectives separate the truth from the lies? (S2 Ep1)[S]

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise holiday is your greatest one!

programme.title: Teleshopping Block
programme.desc: Shop by phone from the comfort of your home.

programme.title: Billion Dollar Cruise Ship...
programme.desc: ...Below Deck: Expensive Cruise Ships. Documentary following the Seven Seas Explorer. Expectations are high as the ship docks at the popular Baltic cruise destination of St Petersburg. (S1 Ep4)[S]

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel has a disturbing dream about Joey. Phoebe camps out in front of Monica's restaurant. (S9 Ep19) [S]

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel struggles with her feelings for Joey. Meanwhile, Joey has given the gang tickets to a play. (S9 Ep20) [S]

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler and Monica secretly go to a fertility clinic. Ross helps Joey to charm Charlie, even though he wants to date her as well. (S9 Ep21) [AD,S]

programme.title: **Visually Signed**The Highland...
programme.desc: ...Vet. Observational documentary series following vets in Thurso and Wick. A pregnant Highland cow has serious complications. (S2 Ep8)[S]

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: **Visually Signed** Milo
programme.desc: When Milo offers to help news reporter Harriet find a front-page headline for The Gazette, the gang must find an exciting news story. (VS) (S1 Ep2)[S]

programme.title: **Visually Signed** Mixmups
programme.desc: The Mixmups go on an Easter Egg hunting adventure, but when Pockets finds all the eggs, can they find another way for them ALL to fill their baskets? (VS) (Ep13)[AD,S]

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit shows Peppa's family around the Botanical Gardens.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mr Potato visits the school to talk about fruit and vegetables. But which team will win his quiz?

programme.title: Milkshake! Monkey
programme.desc: Preschool series starring an inquisitive puppet. Milkshake Monkey joins lots of friends for a gymnastics class!

programme.title: Milo
programme.desc: Milo, Lofty and Lark offer to write Commander Cottontail's biography, following him through the jungle on an exciting expedition. But then they lose him...

programme.title: Thomas & Friends: All Engines Go!
programme.desc: With the holidays just around the corner, Thomas and Percy are determined to deliver the mail. But there's a massive snowstorm preventing the engines from seeing where they're going.

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: After Norman crashes the submersible while trying to video the Pontypandyness Monster, his footage on the local news sparks monster mania in town!

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.desc: The Browns have new neighbours, and Toq is full of tales - like there's a goblin in the garden, and her dad is an astronaut! They can't be true, can they?

programme.title: Tim Rex in Space
programme.desc: Tim tries to relax, but Tia has other ideas.

programme.title: Pip and Posy
programme.desc: Pip and Posy are drawing ducklings, but an accident leaves Pip's yellow creation looking green. Will it be fun to try some different colours?

programme.title: Pip and Posy
programme.desc: Posy gets Pip's kite stuck in a tree when he isn't looking, and tries to cover up by telling him a fib. When they can't find any trace of the kite, it's time for Posy to own up.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is playing with Candy Cat when Emily Elephant's Granny moves out of the apartment. When the new neighbours arrive, Peppa recognises her holiday friend Rohan Rhino.

programme.title: PAW Patrol
programme.desc: Encased in a huge chocolate bunny, Humdinger is carried away by hungry raccoons and ends up floating in Adventure Bay with a curious dolphin.

programme.title: PAW Patrol
programme.desc: When a magic trick goes awry, the Adventure Bay Easter Festival is overrun by wild bunnies.

programme.title: Animal Care Club
programme.desc: The Cadets visit Grace Lane Vets for a day of animal care! They brush Riley the dog's teeth to keep his smile sparkling, give a leopard tortoise a health check, and help look after a little lamb called Brittany.

programme.title: The Smurfs
programme.desc: Rowena casts a spell on Voltaire forcing him to obey and depriving Lily of her dragon friend. (S3 Ep5)

programme.title: The Creature Cases
programme.desc: When a dung beetle couple's cherished dung ball goes missing, Sam and Kit embark on a wild (and smelly!) chase through the African desert to find it! (S1 Ep10)

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: The group decides to go to a convention dedicated to food, but things go wrong when they become stranded in the woods.

programme.title: Entertainment News on 5
programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Walking Victorian Britain
programme.desc: Onyeka Nubia explores Edinburgh. As far back as 1437 it was named capital of Scotland. A city that reinvented itself for the Victorian Age as a place of science, commerce and academia.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: Brighton's Royal Pavilion was built by Prince Regent, George IV in 1787. He took a small beach house and turned it into a flamboyant multi-domino-Saracenic wonder palace.

programme.title: Secrets of the Royal Palaces
programme.desc: Kensington Palace had always been a party palace but a gap in succession made way for foreign import of George I of Hanover and his refurbishments.

programme.title: Inside The Hilton: Park Lane
programme.desc: The inside story of one of the world's most luxurious hotels. General Manager Matthew Mullan hopes building work on the new lobby will be finished ready for King Charles's Coronation...

programme.title: Inside The Hilton: Park Lane
programme.desc: As the multi-million-pound renovation continues, the sales team welcome a foreign dignitary, the kitchen team prepare a banquet for 800 VIPs and emotional stories of staff and a special guest are revealed.

programme.title: Carol Klein's Summer Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Klein welcomes us back to Glebe Cottage garden, where the hot summer weather is parching the open, sun-bathed parts of the garden, whilst the shady areas remain cool.

programme.title: Carol Klein's Summer Gardening
programme.desc: As summer reaches its climax at Glebe Cottage garden in Devon, Carol Klein introduces us to a group of plants that really provides the drum roll to peak summer time: the daisy.

programme.title: Yorkshire Great and Small with Dan and Helen
programme.desc: Dan and Helen follow in the footsteps of All Creatures Great and Small author James Herriot as they explore the breathtaking natural beauty and uncover the hidden delights of Swaledale and Wensleydale.

programme.title: Yorkshire Great and Small with Dan and Helen
programme.desc: Dan and Helen complete their tour, exploring the mystery of the vanishing village at Wharram Percy and visiting the modern marvel of the Humber Bridge

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Cornwall: A Year by the Sea
programme.desc: A volunteer RNLI lifeboat crew head out on a demanding training exercise to prepare for unpredictable sea conditions. And the Cornish Seal Sanctuary rescue team care for injured and abandoned seal pups.

programme.title: Secrets of Diana's Wedding Dress
programme.desc: Explore the creation of Princess Diana's legendary wedding dress through exclusive access to designer Elizabeth Emanuel, rare archives, and captivating stories of the secrecy, glamour, and transformation.

programme.title: Diana: The Princess and the Bodyguard
programme.desc: Ken Wharfe, Princess Diana's royal protection officer - and confidante - reveals the intimate story of the bond he shared with the most famous woman in the world.

programme.title: When TV Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: Anita Dobson narrates a look back at some 80s TV moments that many celebrities would rather forget, from a stunt that went wrong for Paul Daniels to Des O'Connor's dog bite on the nose.

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Hits
programme.desc: A celebratory Top 30 countdown of the biggest-selling UK singles of the 80s - from The Human League to Kylie Minogue, Frankie Goes to Hollywood to Madonna, and Michael Jackson to David Bowie.

programme.title: Tangerine
programme.desc: It's Christmas Eve in Tinseltown and Sin-Dee (newcomer Kitana Kiki Rodriguez) is back on the block.

programme.title: Swan Song
programme.desc: A retired hairdresser Pat, escapes his small-town nursing home after learning of his former client's dying wish for him to style her final hairdo. Pat embarks on a comical and bittersweet odyssey across town to confront the ghosts of his past.

programme.title: Touchy Feely
programme.desc: A massage therapist suddenly develops an aversion to bodily contact.

programme.title: HOW I LIVE NOW
programme.desc: An American teenager sent to stay with relatives in the English countryside finds her idyllic summer days shattered as a conflict in Europe escalates, and the UK falls into a chaotic military state.

programme.title: Night Catches Us
programme.desc: An ex-black panther returns home to find himself right back into the rivalries and love affair he left behind.

programme.title: Riders of Justice
programme.desc: Recently deployed Markus returns home to care for his daughter after his wife is killed in an accident. But, Markus begins to suspect his wife was murdered and embarks on a mission of revenge.

programme.title: Bronson
programme.desc: In 1974, a misguided 19 year old named Michael Peterson decided he wanted to make a name for himself and so, he attempted to rob a post office.

programme.title: Blackthorn
programme.desc: Sam Shepard is Butch Cassidy in this western about the legendary outlaw's return from exile.

programme.title: Frontera
programme.desc: Miguel crosses the Arizona-Mexican border illegally and is accused of murdering the wife of a former sheriff. His pregnant wife gets involved with Mexican smugglers trying to help him.

programme.title: Angels Crest
programme.desc: The disappearance of a 3-year-old boy divides a tightly knit community and culls out its secrets.

programme.title: Fay Grim
programme.desc: A single mother from Queens becomes unwittingly embroiled in international espionage.

programme.title: Best Night Ever
programme.desc: Bride-to-be Claire, her sister Leslie, fun-loving Zoe, and quirky new friend Janet set off to Las Vegas for a one-night bachelorette party that turns out to be more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Results
programme.desc: Recently divorced, newly rich, and utterly miserable, Danny [Kevin Corrigan] would seem to be the perfect test subject for a definitive look at the relationship between money and happiness.

programme.title: The Right Kind of Wrong
programme.desc: Leo Palamino is a failed-writer-turned-dishwasher made famous for his many flaws and shortcomings in a blog called "Why You Suck," a huge Internet success written by his ex-wife.

programme.title: Skate Kitchen
programme.desc: Camille's life as a lonely suburban teenager changes when she befriends a group of girl skateboarders.

programme.title: Support the Girls
programme.desc: The general manager at a highway-side ''sports bar with curves" is tested over the course of a long day.

programme.title: Take This Waltz
programme.desc: A happily married woman falls for the artist who lives across the street.

programme.title: First Date
programme.desc: Mike summons the courage to ask out his neighbor Kelsey, but doesn't have a car. He’s conned into buying a beat up Chrysler and his plan to win over Kelsey soon turns into a surreal misadventure.

programme.title: The Great Buck Howard
programme.desc: Once upon a time, Buck Howard (John Malkovich) spent his days in the limelight as a mentalist extraordinaire! Nowadays, it's clear to everyone except Buck that his act has lost its luster.

programme.title: I Give It A Year
programme.desc: Since they met at a party, ambitious high-flyer Nat and struggling novelist Josh have been deliriously happy despite their differences.

programme.title: Barry Munday
programme.desc: Barry is s missing his family jewels. To make matters worse, he learns he's facing a paternity lawsuit.

programme.title: The Magic of Belle Isle
programme.desc: Morgan Freeman plays Monte Wildhorn, a famous Western novelist whose passion for writing hits an impasse.

programme.title: The Lost City
programme.desc: Veteran actor Andy Garcia steps into the director's chair for his first voyage into feature filmmaking with this heartfelt tribute to revolutionary, late-'50s-era Cuba featuring Dustin Hoffman.

programme.title: A Royal Affair
programme.desc: An intriguing love triangle between the Danish King, the royal physician and young Queen Caroline Mathilde.

programme.title: Italian Studies
programme.desc: After a writer (Academy Award®-nominee Vanessa Kirby) loses her memory, she finds herself on the streets of New York City. She connects with a group of teenagers — in conversations both real and imagined — searching for a way home.

programme.title: Broken English
programme.desc: A thirty-something meets a Frenchman who shows her just what an amazing place the world can truly be.

programme.title: Best Man Down
programme.desc: The best man dies at the wedding and the bride and groom end up on an adventure planning his funeral.

programme.title: A Taste of Hunger
programme.desc: A power couple within the Danish gourmet scene run the popular restaurant Malus in Copenhagen. The couple is willing to sacrifice everything to achieve their dream – getting the coveted Michelin star.

programme.title: Experimenter
programme.desc: Yale University, 1961. Stanley Milgram (Peter Sarsgaard) designs a psychology experiment that remains relevant to this day.

programme.title: Force Majeure
programme.desc: A wickedly funny psychodrama of a model Swedish family—Tomas, his wife and their children— on a skiing holiday. An avalanche suddenly bears down on them. Tomas makes a decision that leaves him struggling to reclaim his role as family patriarch.

programme.title: Two Lovers
programme.desc: After falling for two very different women in quick succession, a bachelor struggles deciding who he loves.

programme.title: Lemon
programme.desc: Lemon: a person or thing that proves to be defective, imperfect, or unsatisfactory.

programme.title: Person to Person
programme.desc: Follows a variety of New York characters as they navigate personal relationships and unexpected problems.

programme.title: I'm Still Here
programme.desc: Joaquin Phoenix, an out-of-control Hollywood celebrity, commits career suicide when he announces his retirement from acting to pursue a hip-hop career.

programme.title: Bad Milo
programme.desc: A guy learns that his unusual stomach problems are being caused by a demon living in his intestines.

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: A helplessly romantic sociopath escapes from a mental institution and invades the home of a ‘90s pop superstar. He just wants to be loved; she just wants to survive.

programme.title: Unlocked
programme.desc: After failing to apprehend the terrorist behind a Paris attack that claimed dozens of lives, CIA agent Alice Racine is forced to live in London as a caseworker. Her mentor unexpectedly calls her back into action when the CIA discovers that another attack is imminent. Alice soon learns that the classified information she's uncovered has been compromised. Running for her life, she turns to a former soldier to help her prevent a lethal biological attack on the citizens of London.

programme.title: Leave No Trace
programme.desc: A father and daughter live a perfect but mysterious existence in Forest Park, a beautiful nature reserve near Portland, Ore., rarely making contact with the world. But when a small mistake tips them off to authorities, they are sent on an increasingly erratic journey in search of a place to call their own.

programme.title: Cleaner
programme.desc: Hoping to expose corruption, radical activists take 300 hostages at an energy company's annual gala in a high-rise building. Their cause soon gets hijacked by an extremist within their ranks who's ready to kill everyone. It's now up to a former soldier who works as a window cleaner to save the hostages trapped inside, including her younger brother.

programme.title: Bring Her Back
programme.desc: A brother and sister uncover a terrifying ritual at the secluded home of their new foster mother.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: A lavish trip through Europe quickly unfolds into a race against time to solve a murder aboard a train. When an avalanche stops the Orient Express dead in its tracks, the world's greatest detective -- Hercule Poirot -- arrives to interrogate all passengers and search for clues before the killer can strike again.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Left on the doorstep of an orphanage run by nuns, three newborn knuckleheads grow up to be finger-poking, nyuk-nyuking janitors named Larry (Sean Hayes), Curly (Will Sasso) and Moe (Chris Diamantopoulos). When they learn that financial problems will soon force the only home they've ever known to close, the trio sets out to raise $830,000 in one month. Out in the world for the very first time, the three innocent bumblers become embroiled in a murder plot and find stardom on a TV reality show.

programme.title: Tyler Perry's Madea's Witness Protection
programme.desc: For years, mild-mannered Wall Street banker George Needleman (Eugene Levy) has meandered through life oblivious to his family's dysfunction and his company's malfeasance, but he's forced to wake up when he learns that he's been framed in a mob-backed Ponzi scheme. Placed under federal protection, George and his family are shipped down South to Madea's (Tyler Perry) house, where the no-nonsense matriarch whips them all into shape using her special brand of tough love.

programme.title: Sound of My Voice
programme.desc: California couple Peter (Christopher Denham) and Lorna (Nicole Vicius) set out to make a documentary meant to expose a charismatic cult leader named Maggie (Brit Marling) as a fraud. Maggie claims to be from the year 2054 and is seemingly allergic to almost everything in her environment; she lives in a basement, subsisting on canned oxygen, homegrown food and the blood of her acolytes. Though at first, Peter and Lorna are adamant that Maggie is a fake, they both soon fall under her spell.

programme.title: Ruby Sparks
programme.desc: Young author Calvin Weir-Fields (Paul Dano), once a literary darling, is having trouble composing his next novel. Following a therapist's advice, Calvin pulls out an old manual typewriter and creates a vivacious, flame-haired woman he dubs Ruby Sparks (Zoe Kazan). Overnight, Ruby leaps from the page into Calvin's home as a real flesh-and-blood woman. And, what's more, she's unaware that she's actually a fictional character and that her actions and feelings are dictated by whatever Calvin writes.

programme.title: Gifted
programme.desc: Frank Adler (Chris Evans) is a single man raising a child prodigy - his spirited young niece Mary (Mckenna Grace) - in a coastal town in Florida. Frank's plans for a normal school life for Mary are foiled when the 7-year-old's mathematical abilities come to the attention of Frank's formidable mother, Evelyn (Lindsay Duncan), whose plans for her granddaughter threaten to separate Frank and Mary.

programme.title: The Peanut Butter Falcon
programme.desc: After running away from a residential nursing home to pursue his dream of becoming a pro wrestler, a man who has Down syndrome befriends an outlaw who becomes his coach and ally.

programme.title: Pan
programme.desc: Living a bleak existence at a London orphanage, 12-year-old Peter finds himself whisked away to the fantastical world of Neverland. Adventure awaits as he meets new friend James Hook and the warrior Tiger Lily. They must band together to save Neverland from the ruthless pirate Blackbeard.

programme.title: Christmas With the Kranks
programme.desc: Finally alone for the holidays, Luther (Tim Allen) and Nora Krank (Jamie Lee Curtis) plan to eschew the Christmas traditions and take a cruise in the Caribbean instead. This doesn't sit well with their Christmas-obsessed neighbors Vic Frohmeyer (Dan Aykroyd) and Walt Scheel (M. Emmet Walsh), who are determined to win the annual "best decorated street" competition, and the Kranks soon find themselves social outcasts because of their lack of Christmas spirit.

programme.title: Isle of Dogs
programme.desc: When, by executive decree, all the canine pets of Megasaki City are exiled to a vast garbage-dump called Trash Island, 12-year-old Atari sets off alone in a miniature Junior-Turbo Prop and flies across the river in search of his bodyguard-dog, Spots. There, with the assistance of a pack of newly-found mongrel friends, he begins an epic journey that will decide the fate and future of the entire Prefecture.

programme.title: Brian Banks
programme.desc: The inspirational true story of Brian Banks, an all-American high school football star who finds his life upended when he's wrongly convicted of a crime he didn't commit. Despite the lack of evidence, Banks gets railroaded through a broken justice system and sentenced to a decade of prison and probation. Years later, with the support of Justin Brooks and the California Innocence Project, Banks fights to reclaim his life and fulfill his dreams of playing in the NFL.

programme.title: Couples Retreat
programme.desc: Four couples, all friends, descend on a tropical island resort. Though one husband and wife are there to work on their marriage, the others just want to enjoy some fun in the sun. They soon find out that the paradise comes at a price, participation in couples therapy sessions is mandatory. What started out as a cut-rate vacation turns into an examination of the common problems many face.

programme.title: Going the Distance
programme.desc: Erin (Drew Barrymore) and Garrett (Justin Long) are very much in love. When Erin moves to San Francisco to finish her journalism degree and Garrett stays behind in New York to work in the music industry, they gamely keep the romance alive with webcams and frequent-flyer miles. But just when it seems the lovers will soon be reunited, they each score a big break that could separate them for good.

programme.title: It's Kind of a Funny Story
programme.desc: Stressed by adolescence, 16-year-old Craig Gilner (Keir Gilchrist) checks himself into a mental-health clinic. Unfortunately, the youth wing is closed, so he must spend his mandated five-day stay with adults. One of them, Bobby (Zach Galifianakis), quickly becomes his mentor -- and protege, while Craig finds himself drawn to a fellow teen, Noelle (Emma Roberts), who just may be the cure he needs to forget an unrequited crush.

programme.title: Mid90s
programme.desc: In the 1990s Los Angeles, a 13-year-old spends his summer navigating between a troubled home life and a crew of new friends he meets at a skate shop.

programme.title: Oracle
programme.desc: A young woman accepts a job on a property with a traumatic past. Upon her arrival, she soon realizes that the nightmares of her childhood are connected to the evil in the house.

programme.title: The Watch
programme.desc: In Glenview, Ohio, four pals, Evan, Bob, Franklin and Jamarcus, form a neighborhood watch group. Armed with flashlights, walkie-talkies and spiffy new jackets, the guys try to take their mission seriously, even if they spend a little time goofing off now and then. But when they discover aliens living among them and no one believes their story, they learn that they have to kick things up a notch to save the world from annihilation.

programme.title: The Man
programme.desc: Seeking the gunrunners who killed his partner, undercover cop Derrick Vann (Samuel L. Jackson) sets up a sting. He arranges to meet an arms dealer at a diner, where he'll be the only one reading USA Today. But dentist Andy Fiddler (Eugene Levy), who's in Detroit for a conference, is there reading the same paper, and the dealer mistakes him for Vann and sets up the deal. When Vann realizes this, he drags the baffled Andy into the operation, forcing him to pose as the fake buyer.

programme.title: Colette
programme.desc: After moving to Paris, author Sidonie-Gabrielle Colette agrees to ghostwrite a semi-autobiographical novel for her husband. Its success soon inspires her to fight for creative ownership and overcome the societal constraints of the early 20th century.

programme.title: Margaret
programme.desc: New York high-school student Lisa Cohen (Anna Paquin) inadvertently causes an accident in which a bus driver (Mark Ruffalo) runs over a pedestrian (Allison Janney). Guilt-stricken over her role in the woman's death, Lisa's mood swings from normal to furious, with her angry outbursts mostly directed at her mother (J. Smith-Cameron). Lisa reaches out to the dead woman's best friend (Jeannie Berlin) and the bus driver, but her failed efforts to make amends only lead to more hostility.

programme.title: Love Happens
programme.desc: Dr. Burke Ryan (Aaron Eckhart) is a self-help guru who is on the verge of a lucrative multimedia deal. But there's a problem: though he advises his patients to confront their pain, Burke has never come to terms with his wife's death. Eloise Chandler (Jennifer Aniston) has had enough of men and dating, and is busy concentrating on her floral business. When they meet, Burke and Eloise feel an instant attraction, but wonder if they'll be able to let go of their painful pasts and embrace the future.

programme.title: It's Complicated
programme.desc: Jane (Meryl Streep), a successful restaurateur, has been divorced from Jake (Alec Baldwin) for many years, although they remain friends. When an innocent meal together turns into an affair, Jane finds that she is the other woman in the now-remarried Jake's life. Meanwhile, Adam (Steve Martin), an architect hired to remodel her kitchen, is still stinging from his own divorce. He finds himself falling in love with Jane but soon realizes that he has become part of a triangle.

programme.title: The Family
programme.desc: After ratting out his Mafia cohorts, Giovanni Manzoni (Robert De Niro) and his family enter the Witness Protection Program and relocate to a French town. Despite the best efforts of their handler (Tommy Lee Jones) to keep them in line, Giovanni (now called Fred Blake), his wife (Michelle Pfeiffer) and children (Dianna Agron, John D'Leo) can't help but resort to doing things the "family" way. However, their dependence on such old habits places everyone in danger from vengeful mobsters.

programme.title: Broken City
programme.desc: Former cop Billy Taggart (Mark Wahlberg) sees a chance at redemption for past sins when New York City's Mayor Nicolas Hostetler (Russell Crowe) calls on him for a special job. Hostetler thinks his wife, Cathleen (Catherine Zeta-Jones), is having an affair and that it may hurt his chances for re-election. However, a bigger scandal than expected pulls in Billy right at the start of his investigation. Double-crossed by the mayor, Billy begins a relentless quest for justice.

programme.title: The Post
programme.desc: Katharine Graham is the first female publisher of a major American newspaper -- The Washington Post. With help from editor Ben Bradlee, Graham races to catch up with The New York Times to expose a massive cover-up of government secrets that spans three decades and four U.S. presidents. Together, they must overcome their differences as they risk their careers -- and very freedom -- to help bring long-buried truths to light.

programme.title: Sunshine Cleaning
programme.desc: Though they once seemed to have a bright future, life seems to be passing by the Norkowski sisters. Rose (Amy Adams), once the most popular girl in high school, is a single mom and works as a maid. Norah (Emily Blunt) still lives at home with their father (Alan Arkin) and parties all the time. When Rose's married lover tells her about the lucrative field of crime-scene cleanup, she pours all her remaining resources into her new business, and recruits the squeamish Norah to help.

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: "Fargo" is a reality-based crime drama set in Minnesota in 1987. Jerry Lundegaard (William H. Macy) is a car salesman in Minneapolis who has gotten himself into debt and is so desperate for money that he hires two thugs (Steve Buscemi), (Peter Stormare) to kidnap his own wife. Jerry will collect the ransom from her wealthy father (Harve Presnell), paying the thugs a small portion and keeping the rest to satisfy his debts. The scheme collapses when the thugs shoot a state trooper.

programme.title: The Hole in the Ground
programme.desc: One night, Sarah's young son disappears into the woods behind their rural home. When he returns, he looks the same, but his behavior grows increasingly disturbing. Sarah begins to believe that the boy who returned may not be her son at all.

programme.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure
programme.desc: Thomas leads some escaped Gladers on their final and most dangerous mission yet. To save their friends, they must break into the legendary Last City, a WCKD-controlled labyrinth that may turn out to be the deadliest maze of all. Anyone who makes it out alive will get answers to the questions that the Gladers have been asking since they arrived in the maze.

programme.title: We Live in Time
programme.desc: A surprise encounter brings together a couple, changing the course of their lives. Challenged by the limits of time, they learn to cherish each moment of their decade-spanning and movingly rare love story.

programme.title: The Mountain Between Us
programme.desc: Stranded on a mountain after a tragic plane crash, two strangers must work together to endure the extreme elements of the remote, snow-covered terrain. Realizing that help is not on the way, they embark on a perilous journey across hundreds of miles of wilderness, pushing each other to survive and discovering their inner strength.

programme.title: The 33
programme.desc: Disaster strikes on Aug. 5, 2010, as a copper and gold mine collapses in Chile, trapping 33 men underground. With more than 2,000 feet of rock in their way, members of a rescue team work tirelessly for 69 days to save the seemingly doomed crew. Beneath the rubble, the miners begin an epic quest to survive, contending with suffocating heat and the need for food and water. With family, friends and the rest of the world watching, it becomes a race against time and a true test of the human spirit.

programme.title: It
programme.desc: In 1960, seven preteen outcasts fight an evil demon that poses as a child-killing clown. Thirty years later, they reunite to stop the demon once and for all when it returns to their hometown.

programme.title: Horizon: An American Saga: Chapter 1
programme.desc: In 1859 families discover the lure of the Old West as they settle in territories from Wyoming to Kansas. Meanwhile, a gruff cowboy soon finds himself on the run with a prostitute and a young boy after killing a fellow gunman.

programme.title: Tomb Raider
programme.desc: Lara Croft is the fiercely independent daughter of an eccentric adventurer who vanished years earlier. Hoping to solve the mystery of her father's disappearance, Croft embarks on a perilous journey to his last-known destination -- a fabled tomb on a mythical island that might be somewhere off the coast of Japan. The stakes couldn't be higher as Lara must rely on her sharp mind, blind faith and stubborn spirit to venture into the unknown.

programme.title: Due Date
programme.desc: Peter Highman will be a dad for the first time when his wife gives birth in five days. He intends to catch a flight home from Atlanta so he can be there for the delivery, but a chance encounter with aspiring actor Ethan Tremblay throws a monkey wrench into his plans. Desperate to reach his wife before their baby is born, Peter's sanity is tested when he must take a road trip cross-country with dog-toting Ethan.

programme.title: Final Destination
programme.desc: Alex Browning is embarking on a trip to Paris. He experiences a premonition -- he sees the plane explode moments after leaving the ground. Insisting that everyone get off the plane, Alex and seven other people are forced to disembark. All watch as the plane actually explodes in a fireball. He and the other survivors have briefly cheated death, but will not be able to evade their fate for very long. One by one, these fugitives from fate fall victim to the grim reaper.

programme.title: Final Destination 2
programme.desc: Kimberly (A.J. Cook) has a premonition of a horrible highway accident killing multiple people -- including her and her friends. She blocks the cars behind her on the ramp from joining traffic -- and as a police trooper (Michael Landes) arrives, the accident actually happens. Now, Death is stalking this group of mistaken survivors -- and one by one they are dying as they were supposed to on the highway.

programme.title: Final Destination 3
programme.desc: Six years after a group of high-school students first cheated death, another teen (Mary Elizabeth Winstead) has a premonition that she and her friends will be involved in a horrifying roller-coaster accident. When the vision proves true, the student and her fellow survivors must deal with the repercussions of cheating the Grim Reaper.

programme.title: Final Destination 5
programme.desc: During a bus ride with his colleagues to a corporate retreat, Sam (Nicholas D'Agosto) experiences a horrifying vision: the suspension bridge that they -- and many others -- are crossing starts to crumble around them. When his vision ends and, almost immediately, starts to come true, Sam takes quick action that saves a number of people, including his girlfriend, Molly (Emma Bell), and his best friend, Peter (Miles Fisher). However, the survivors soon find that Death will not be denied.

programme.title: The Final Destination
programme.desc: While enjoying a day at the track, Nick O'Bannon (Bobby Campo) has a horrific premonition of his friends and him all dying in a freak accident involving many racecars. Mere seconds before the vision comes true, he manages to convince them to leave. Although they cheat death then, the survivors each begin to meet a grisly end, and Nick tries to figure a way to escape a similar fate.

programme.title: Miss You Already
programme.desc: A woman (Toni Collette) undergoes treatment to battle breast cancer while her best friend (Drew Barrymore) tries to get pregnant.

programme.title: Carol
programme.desc: Aspiring photographer Therese spots the beautiful, elegant Carol perusing the doll displays in a 1950s Manhattan department store. The two women develop a fast bond that becomes a love with complicated consequences.

programme.title: Snatched
programme.desc: Dumped by her boyfriend on the eve of their vacation, impetuous dreamer Emily Middleton persuades her cautious mother, Linda, to accompany her on an exotic getaway to South America. Polar opposites, Emily and Linda must soon work through their differences to escape from a wildly outrageous and dangerous jungle adventure.

programme.title: The Hitman's Bodyguard
programme.desc: The world's top protection agent is called upon to guard the life of his mortal enemy, one of the world's most notorious hit men. The relentless bodyguard and manipulative assassin have been on the opposite end of the bullet for years and are thrown together for a wildly outrageous 24 hours. During their journey from England to the Hague, they encounter high-speed car chases, outlandish boat escapades and a merciless Eastern European dictator who is out for blood.

programme.title: The Hitman's Wife's Bodyguard
programme.desc: Bodyguard Michael Bryce and hitman Darius Kincaid are back for another life-threatening mission; still unlicensed and under scrutiny, Bryce is forced into action by Darius's even more volatile wife; soon, all three are in over their heads when a madman's sinister plot threatens to leave Europe in total chaos.

programme.title: Green Lantern
programme.desc: Sworn to preserve intergalactic order, the Green Lantern Corps has existed for centuries. Its newest recruit, Hal Jordan, is the first human to join the ranks. The Green Lanterns have little regard for humans, who have thus far been unable to harness the powers of the ring each member wears. But Jordan, a gifted and cocky test pilot, may be the corps' only hope when a new enemy called Parallax threatens the universal balance of power.

programme.title: Wonder Woman
programme.desc: Before she is Wonder Woman, she is Diana, princess of the Amazons, trained to be an unconquerable warrior. Raised on a sheltered island paradise, Diana meets an American pilot who tells her about the conflict that's raging in the outside world. Convinced that she can stop the threat, Diana leaves her home for the first time. Fighting alongside men in a war to end all wars, she finally discovers her full powers and true destiny.

programme.title: Wonder Woman 1984
programme.desc: Diana Prince lives quietly among mortals in the vibrant, sleek 1980s -- an era of excess driven by the pursuit of having it all. Though she's come into her full powers, she maintains a low profile by curating ancient artifacts, and only performing heroic acts incognito. But soon, Diana will have to muster all of her strength, wisdom and courage as she finds herself squaring off against Maxwell Lord and the Cheetah, a villainess who possesses superhuman strength and agility.

programme.title: Justice League
programme.desc: Fueled by his restored faith in humanity and inspired by Superman's selfless act, Bruce Wayne enlists newfound ally Diana Prince to face an even greater threat. Together, Batman and Wonder Woman work quickly to recruit a team to stand against this newly awakened enemy. Despite the formation of an unprecedented league of heroes -- Batman, Wonder Woman, Aquaman, Cyborg and the Flash -- it may be too late to save the planet from an assault of catastrophic proportions.

programme.title: The Asphalt Jungle
programme.desc: Recently released from prison, Dix Handley concocts a plan to steal $1 million in jewels. Dix gathers a team of small-time crooks, including a safecracker and a lawyer, and the heist is a success until a stray bullet kills one of the men. As they scramble to pick up the pieces after the theft, the men let their greed get the best of them while entangling themselves in webs of deceit, treachery and murder.

programme.title: The Great Wall
programme.desc: When a mercenary warrior (Matt Damon) is imprisoned within the Great Wall, he discovers the mystery behind one of the greatest wonders of the world. As wave after wave of marauding beasts besiege the massive structure, his quest for fortune turns into a journey toward heroism as he joins a huge army of elite warriors to confront the unimaginable and seemingly unstoppable force.

programme.title: Raise Hell: The Life & Times of Molly Ivins
programme.desc: The life and work of Molly Ivins, a political journalist from Texas, best-selling author, and Bill of Rights warrior.

programme.title: Woodshock
programme.desc: A haunted young woman spirals into confusion and chaos after she falls under the spell of a potent, reality-altering drug.

programme.title: Walk the Line
programme.desc: The rise of country music legend Johnny Cash (Joaquin Phoenix) begins with his days as a boy growing up on the family farm, where he struggles under the scorn of his father (Robert Patrick). As the years pass, Cash ends up in Memphis, Tenn., with his wife, Vivian (Ginnifer Goodwin), and breaks into the music scene after finding his trademark sound. While on tour, Cash meets the love of his life, singer June Carter (Reese Witherspoon), but Cash's volatile lifestyle threatens to keep them apart.

programme.title: Dim Sum Funeral
programme.desc: In the wake of her death, the embittered children of Chinese-American matriarch Mrs. Xiao (Lisa Lu) begrudgingly return to their childhood home for a bristly -- and brief, they hope -- reunion. But after Xiao's maid and sole confidant, Viola (Talia Shire), reveals her wish to undergo a week-long Buddhist funeral rite, the younger Xiaos -- including plastic surgeon Alexander (Russell Wong) and lesbian actress Meimei (Steph Song) -- have seven days to come to terms with her death, and each other.

programme.title: Love, Simon
programme.desc: Everyone deserves a great love story, but for 17-year-old Simon Spier, it's a little more complicated. He hasn't told his family or friends that he's gay, and he doesn't know the identity of the anonymous classmate that he's fallen for online. Resolving both issues proves hilarious, terrifying and life-changing.

programme.title: Lady Bird
programme.desc: A teenager (Saoirse Ronan) navigates a loving but turbulent relationship with her strong-willed mother (Laurie Metcalf) over the course of an eventful and poignant senior year of high school.

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 3
programme.desc: After the highs of winning the world championships, the Bellas find themselves split apart and discovering there aren't job prospects for making music with your mouth. But when they get the chance to reunite for an overseas USO tour, the group of nerds comes together to make some music one last time.

programme.title: Red Sparrow
programme.desc: Prima ballerina Dominika Egorova faces a bleak and uncertain future after she suffers an injury that ends her career. She soon turns to Sparrow School, a secret intelligence service that trains exceptional young people to use their minds and bodies as weapons. Egorova emerges as the most dangerous Sparrow after completing the sadistic training process. As she comes to terms with her new abilities, Dominika meets a CIA agent who tries to convince her that he is the only person she can trust.

programme.title: Kingsman: The Golden Circle
programme.desc: With their headquarters destroyed and the world held hostage, members of Kingsman find new allies when they discover a spy organization in the United States known as Statesman. In an adventure that tests their strength and wits, the elite secret agents from both sides of the pond band together to battle a ruthless enemy and save the day, something that's becoming a bit of a habit for Eggsy.

programme.title: Catfish
programme.desc: When Nev Shulman, a New York photographer, receives a Facebook request from a child named Abby, he cannot guess the unusual odyssey on which he will soon embark. Abby sends Nev an unusually skilled painting based on one of his photos. Intrigued, he begins online relationships with the rest of Abby's family, including her older sister, Megan. Though troubling inconsistencies soon emerge, Nev continues his online flirtation with Megan, resulting in a road trip to meet her in person.

programme.title: The Town
programme.desc: Doug MacRay leads a band of ruthless bank robbers and has no real attachments except for James, who -- despite his dangerous temper -- is like a brother. Everything changes for Doug when James briefly takes a hostage, bank employee Claire Keesey. Learning that she lives in the gang's neighborhood, Doug seeks her out to discover what she knows, and he falls in love. As the romance deepens, he wants out of his criminal life, but that could threaten Claire.

programme.title: All Eyez on Me
programme.desc: The true and untold story of prolific rapper, actor, poet and activist Tupac Shakur (Demetrius Shipp Jr.), from his early days in New York to his status as one of the world's most recognized and influential voices. Against all odds, Shakur's raw talent, powerful lyrics and revolutionary mindset establish him as a cultural icon whose legacy continues to grow long after his death.

programme.title: Everything, Everything
programme.desc: Maddy (Amandla Stenberg) is a smart, curious and imaginative 18-year-old who is unable to leave the protection of the hermetically-sealed environment within her house because of an illness. Olly (Nick Robinson) is the boy next door who won't let that stop them from being together. Gazing through windows and talking only through texts, Maddy and Olly form a deep bond that leads them to risk everything to be together, even if it means losing everything.

programme.title: Gifted
programme.desc: Frank Adler (Chris Evans) is a single man raising a child prodigy - his spirited young niece Mary (Mckenna Grace) - in a coastal town in Florida. Frank's plans for a normal school life for Mary are foiled when the 7-year-old's mathematical abilities come to the attention of Frank's formidable mother, Evelyn (Lindsay Duncan), whose plans for her granddaughter threaten to separate Frank and Mary.

programme.title: Patti Cake$
programme.desc: Fed up with life in her New Jersey hometown, Patricia Dombrowski, aka Patti Cake$, hopes to follow in the footsteps of her idol and hit the road to achieve stardom as a rapper. With help from her best friend, a mysterious musician and her loving grandmother, Patti leads the charge against an army of haters, unpaid bills and the broken dreams that are holding her back.

programme.title: They All Laughed
programme.desc: The employees of New York City's Odyssey Detective Agency can't seem to prevent themselves from getting over-involved in their clients' lives. When aging gumshoe John Russo (Ben Gazzara) is assigned to trail an Italian millionaire's wife, Angela (Audrey Hepburn), he inevitably falls for her continental charm. His partner, Charles (John Ritter), is similarly unable to keep his hands off the woman he's trailing -- a winsome cheating spouse named Dolores (Dorothy Stratten).

programme.title: Look Into My Eyes
programme.desc: A group of New York City psychics conducts deeply intimate readings for their clients, revealing a kaleidoscope of loneliness, connection and healing.

programme.title: A Different Man
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Edward undergoes a radical medical procedure to drastically transform his appearance. But his new dream face turns into a nightmare as he becomes obsessed with reclaiming what he lost.

programme.title: Companion
programme.desc: A weekend getaway at a remote cabin turns to chaos when it's revealed that one of the guests -- a subservient android built for human companionship -- has gone haywire.

programme.title: Red Riding Hood
programme.desc: Young Valerie (Amanda Seyfried) feels torn between two men. She loves Peter, but her parents intend that she marry wealthy Henry. Valerie and Peter are going to run away together, but her sister is killed by a werewolf before they can put their plan into action. The villagers call on the help of Father Solomon (Gary Oldman), a famous monster hunter who warns that the beast takes human form by day. Then Valerie suspects that the werewolf is someone she loves.

programme.title: Sucker Punch
programme.desc: Locked away, a young woman named Babydoll retreats to a fantasy world where she is free to go wherever her mind takes her. Determined to fight for real freedom, she finds four women -- Rocket, Blondie, Amber and Sweet Pea -- to join together to escape the terrible fate that awaits them. With a virtual arsenal at their disposal, the allies battle everything from samurais to serpents, while trying to decide what price they will pay for survival.

programme.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets
programme.desc: In the 28th century, special operatives Valerian and Laureline work together to maintain order throughout the human territories. Under assignment from the minister of defense, the duo embarks on a mission to Alpha, an ever-expanding metropolis where diverse species gather to share knowledge and culture. When a dark force threatens the peaceful city, Valerian and Laureline must race against time to identify the menace that also jeopardizes the future of the universe.

programme.title: It Should Happen to You
programme.desc: (1954) Judy Holliday stars in this comedy as a smalltown girl who comes to New York and rents a billboard to publicise her name - and soon she's a TV star. With Jack Lemmon.

programme.title: The Incredible Shrinking Man
programme.desc: (1957) Classic sci-fi horror about the perils of radiation. A normal-sized fella gets a lot, lot smaller when he breathes in a mysterious mist. Definitely not one for arachnophobes!

programme.title: The Kid Who Would Be King
programme.desc: (2019) Fantasy adventure. A 12-year-old from modern-day suburbia finds King Arthur's sword Excalibur and must save the country from a sinister sorceress. With Patrick Stewart.

programme.title: Ad Astra
programme.desc: (2019) Visually stunning sci-fi starring Brad Pitt as a famously cool astronaut sent to the edges of the solar system to save Earth from deadly power surges. With Tommy Lee Jones.

programme.title: Commando
programme.desc: (1985) Thriller starring Arnold Schwarzenegger. To free his kidnapped daughter, an ex US commando must agree to assassinate a South American president. But he's not as pliant as he seems...

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost...
programme.desc: ...Protocol: (2011) Part four of the Tom Cruise action-thriller franchise. The Impossible Missions Force fight to clear their name, while also trying to avert nuclear war. Busy, busy, busy!

programme.title: Monday
programme.desc: (2020) Romcom-cum-romtradge about what can happen when a steamy holiday fling morphs into something more permanent. And it ain't necessarily good. Starring Sebastian Stan and Denise Gough. [S]

programme.title: The Frogmen
programme.desc: (1951) Second World War thriller. Richard Widmark, Dana Andrews and Robert Wagner star as divers with a special underwater demolition team taking the war to the Japanese in the Pacific. [AD,S]

programme.title: The Man from Colorado
programme.desc: (1948) Western. When a sadistic Union colonel (Glenn Ford) becomes a federal judge after the Civil War, his friend (William Holden) takes the deputy job to try and temper his actions. [AD,S]

programme.title: Anzio
programme.desc: (1968) Epic Second World War drama starring Robert Mitchum. A reporter gets trapped behind enemy lines during the costly 1944 Allied invasion of Italy. With Peter Falk and Robert Ryan. [AD,S]

programme.title: The Old Man and the Gun
programme.desc: (2018) Fact-based crime biopic starring Robert Redford and Sissy Spacek. Charming 74-year-old bank robber Forrest Tucker falls for an enchanting farmer. But is he ready to retire? [AD,S]

programme.title: Terminator: Dark Fate
programme.desc: (2019) Sci-fi action sequel with Linda Hamilton and Arnold Schwarzenegger. A factory worker is targeted by deadly robot assassins from the future. Thank goodness he's back. [AD,S]

programme.title: New Life
programme.desc: (2023) John Rosman's pestilential horror about a young woman running from something that she, perhaps, cannot escape, and the older woman chasing her. Starring Hayley Erin and Sonya Walger.

programme.title: Hotel Splendide
programme.desc: (2000) Comedy with Toni Collette and Daniel Craig. Kath returns to Hotel Splendide, where she used to work as a chef, to rekindle her romance with the son of the hotel's owner.
